description: A vertical, portrait-orientation slogan graphic from the U.S. Department of Labor. The background is a warm, picturesque color photograph of a charming historic urban residential street (evoking Boston's Beacon Hill / Acorn Street): a row of red-brick townhouses with black shutters and window boxes overflowing with greenery and ivy, antique-style black gas lampposts, an American flag mounted and hanging from a pole on a building at right, and a cobblestone street receding into the distance; a small pumpkin sits on a stoop, suggesting autumn. Overlaid across the lower portion in elegant white cursive/script type is the slogan on two lines: 'Restoring the American Dream.' There is no visible agency seal on the image itself; the design relies on idyllic Americana imagery. The messaging technique is nostalgic/aspirational: it associates the administration's economic policies with a wholesome, prosperous, traditional American lifestyle (homeownership, neighborhood, patriotism). Per the tweet, it ties to claims that life is 'becoming more affordable again' under Trump. Target audience is the general public, evoking middle-class aspiration and patriotism.
